resolved_findings.title: Design-spec event vocabulary partially unimplemented
resolved_findings.evidence: Accepted as a Phase 6 scope boundary. The implemented events cover worker lifecycle, polling, job lifecycle, invalid jobs, stop observation, bounds, and worker failure. Startup-failure and heartbeat-written event vocabulary can be reconciled in a future diagnostics cleanup without changing the Phase 6 watch contract.

resolved_findings.title: events.log appends are not fsync'd or atomically renamed
resolved_findings.evidence: Accepted as an observational-artifact durability limitation. The design and handoff already state that scheduler events are local JSONL diagnostics and that long-running retention/rotation remains future work.

resolved_findings.title: SIGINT/SIGTERM leaves heartbeat at transient status
resolved_findings.evidence: Accepted as part of the documented no-stale-recovery boundary. Phase 6 cooperative stop uses stop.json between jobs and does not claim automatic recovery from hard interruption or process termination.

resolved_findings.title: Remote GitHub Actions execution of the new watch package smoke is not verified locally
resolved_findings.evidence: Superseded by post-push CI evidence. GitHub Actions run 27945733032 for codex/phase6-scheduler-watch-mode at f0558c77baefaa7cc5ca9257394dab8a440b48f2 completed successfully, with both test and package-smoke jobs successful. The package-smoke job covered the Ubuntu/bash packaged console-script path including run-scheduler --once and run-scheduler --watch --poll-interval-seconds 0.1 --max-iterations 3.

not_tested: Superseded historical item: Remote GitHub Actions Ubuntu/bash execution of the new watch package-smoke job is now verified by GitHub Actions run 27945733032.
Real non-mocked detached child process lifecycle via start-scheduler.
events.log integrity under concurrent append from two workers.
SIGKILL or power-loss crash recovery semantics for partial events.log lines and stale heartbeat.
events.log size growth and rotation behavior under long-running unbounded watch.
Cloud queue and cross-run queue execution.
Multi-worker claim locking and double-claim prevention.
residual_risks: Double-claim risk remains if two workers are launched against the same run.
Heartbeat is observational only and stale-running jobs are not recovered.
Stop requests are cooperative and do not interrupt running jobs.
A crashed worker can leave heartbeat.json at a non-terminal status.
events.log is append-only with no rotation or fsync.
Detached background process behavior varies by OS shell and terminal.
Superseded historical item: Remote CI for this branch has run and passed as GitHub Actions run 27945733032.
Scheduler subprocess execution inherits the same local permission and environment risks as the generic adapter.
notes: External Claude Code review completed with status findings and no medium, high, or critical findings after follow-up commits fixed the earlier max_seconds and failed-job coverage gaps. Low findings are triaged as documented Phase 6 diagnostics or interruption limitations and do not block reviewed status. Post-push correction: GitHub Actions run 27945733032 for f0558c77baefaa7cc5ca9257394dab8a440b48f2 passed test and package-smoke, superseding the review-time remote-CI-not-run item.
